inputs.creative_direction: Layer de-stacking teardown of Nvidia Spectrum-6 chip. Each layer peels off as a solid slab or a few large grouped pieces -- never as many scattered particles. PACING: layers separate during the first 50-60% of the animation. The final 40% holds ALL layers visibly floating at their separated positions, drifting very slowly apart, giving the viewer time to see the full de-stacked architecture. Do NOT end the animation while layers are still separating. 6 layers separate vertically upward in order: LAYER 1 (top, lifts first): the brushed-metal heat spreader frame with corner mounting brackets detaches as one solid piece, rising straight up to reveal the die beneath. LAYER 2: a thin reflective thermal interface film lifts away like a peel, catching light with a metallic shimmer as it separates. LAYER 3: the central silver silicon ASIC die block (marked NVIDIA SPC6) lifts as a single slab, while the ring of 32 gold-topped silicon photonics CPO modules around the perimeter separate as 4 grouped quadrants (8 modules each) -- drifting apart just enough to reveal the blue substrate between them. LAYER 4: a field of micro-bump interconnects lifts as a single translucent sheet or 4-6 large clustered patches (NOT individual particles) -- giving a brief sparkle of gold before clearing. LAYER 5: the blue CoWoS-L interposer with embedded LSI bridges and glowing copper RDL traces lifts as one solid board, showing the redistribution wiring on its underside. LAYER 6: the dark organic package substrate remains at the bottom with the BGA ball grid visible beneath. Every layer is a solid piece, a grouped cluster, or at most a few large sections -- never dust or scattered tiny parts. CAMERA: slow gentle orbit from the initial 3/4 angle, drifting about 15-20 degrees during the hold phase to reveal depth and spacing between floating layers. Cinematic Nvidia green (#76B900) rim lighting catches each newly exposed edge, dark background.

notes: Run 1: angled 3/4 view of Spectrum-6. De-stacking animation -- 6 layers strip off the chip top-to-bottom. Micro-bumps kept as clustered patches, CPO ring splits into 4 quadrants. Upload reference_angle.png to get URL.
